item.title: pick_item emits spurious error log when auto-mode backlog is drained
item.intent: In auto mode, backlog exhaustion is normal and expected — pick_item must not log an error when the sprint loop will immediately handle the empty result as success.
item.description: When auto done drains the backlog completely, pick_item() at line 734 logs log_error 'No eligible item found in backlog' before returning 1. The sprint loop (line 2704) then correctly prints log_success 'No more ready items — backlog drained'. The spurious red error appears in terminal output right before the green success message, confusing users into thinking something went wrong when the session completed cleanly.
steps: In pick_item(), replace the unconditional log_error on the 'no item found' exit path with a return 1 only — remove the log_error call
Callers already provide their own context-appropriate messages (sprint loop handles auto vs non-auto vs specific-id cases)
Verify: run auto done to exhaust backlog — terminal shows only the success message, no error
Verify: run backlog list on empty-ready backlog — caller still shows warning (cmd_run sprint loop handles this)
acceptanceCriteria: auto done completes with no red error log when all items are done
Non-auto mode still shows the 'No ready items' warning from the sprint loop caller
